description: Dark/Light Mode Toggle - Connect the existing ThemeProvider, add toggle button to Sidebar, and implement CSS variables approach so light mode works without changing every component file
steps: 1. Update frontend/app/globals.css: Expand the :root (light mode) CSS variables section to cover all the colors used by components. Add variables for: --text-primary, --text-secondary, --text-muted, --sidebar-bg, --sidebar-border, --card-text, --border-color. In the .dark section, set corresponding dark values. Update the body, .glass, .glass-card classes to use these CSS variables. Add light-mode-specific overrides for common Tailwind dark classes (slate-950 bg, slate-800 borders, text-white, text-slate-400) using a :root:not(.dark) or html:not(.dark) selector approach.

2. Update frontend/components/dashboard/Sidebar.tsx: Import useTheme from @/lib/theme-context. Add a Sun/Moon toggle button in the SidebarContent between the nav items and the user section at the bottom. Use Sun and Moon icons from lucide-react. When theme is 'dark' show Sun icon (to switch to light), when 'light' show Moon icon (to switch to dark). Style the sidebar using CSS variables instead of hardcoded slate-950/slate-800. The sidebar should use bg-[var(--sidebar-bg)] and border-[var(--sidebar-border)].

3. Update frontend/app/layout.tsx: The layout already has ThemeProvider and the inline script for flash prevention. Verify it works correctly - the html tag starts with className='dark' and the script removes 'dark' and adds 'light' if localStorage has theme=light. This is already implemented correctly - no changes needed unless testing reveals issues.
4. Update frontend/components/dashboard/KPICard.tsx: Change hardcoded text-white to text-[var(--foreground)] and text-slate-400/text-slate-500 to text-[var(--muted)] so it adapts to theme.

5. Add light mode overrides in globals.css for common patterns: html:not(.dark) .glass-card (already partially done), ensure sidebar text colors adapt. The key principle is: dark mode stays as-is since all components already use dark classes, light mode overrides come from CSS.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle button visible in the sidebar with appropriate sun/moon icon
Clicking toggle switches between dark and light themes
Theme persists in localStorage across page reloads
Light mode has readable text on light backgrounds (no white-on-white)
Dark mode still looks the same as before
pnpm build in frontend/ passes without errors
verification: Run pnpm build in frontend/ and confirm it produces 16 routes without errors
findings: Build passes with 16 routes. CSS variables approach works cleanly - dark mode unchanged, light mode gets proper values via :root defaults. ThemeProvider already handles localStorage persistence and class toggling on html element.
